evals.prompt: I need a curl command for our local Shot Scraper API that grabs a dark-mode screenshot of https://example.com at 1440x900 and saves it to a file instead of dumping binary into the terminal.
evals.expected_output: Uses the shots-api skill, chooses a blocking image endpoint, uses the correct query parameter names, and saves the image output to a file.

evals.prompt: Wire my app to enqueue screenshots instead of waiting on them. I want the first request to return a job id, then I can poll until the shot is ready. Show me the HTTP flow against Shot Scraper API.
evals.expected_output: Uses the shots-api skill, selects POST /trigger/shot plus GET /job/{job_id}, and explains how to handle returned relative URLs and queue statuses.

evals.prompt: My screenshot call is failing and I need to debug it. The URL is valid, but I may have passed bad timeout or theme values. Show me how to inspect the status code, response body, and headers from the Shot Scraper API.
evals.expected_output: Uses the shots-api skill, preserves error details, references the documented timeout and theme constraints, and recommends inspecting headers such as Content-Type and X-Screenshot-Status.
